channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Billabong, Quiksilver, Volcom to close all US locations
item.description: Liberated Brands, based in Costa Mesa, California, was the operator and licensee of Billabong, Quiksilver and Volcom in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump is trying to halt the EV charger buildout. Experts say it's not that easy
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Experts are questioning President Donald Trump’s latest effort to slow the electric vehicle charging buildout in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks USAID purge, evacuations from host countries
item.description: A federal judge late Friday temporarily blocked the Trump administration’s plan to place thousands of U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) employees on leave at midnight.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Mystery seed packages return to Texas
item.description: The state’s Department of Agriculture is warning residents not to open the mystery packages and report the items immediately.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, KRON4

item.title: Musk says he'll bring back DOGE staffer under fire for racist posts
item.description: Elon Musk said Friday he will rehire a former Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffer who resigned after several of the aide's racist social media posts were exposed. "He will be brought back," Musk posted on X, the social media platform he purchased in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, NewsNation

item.title: Judge in Boston to consider latest bid to block Trump's birthright citizenship order
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge in Boston said on Friday he would take under advisement a request from 18 state attorneys general to block President Donald Trump’s executive order ending birthright citizenship for the children of parents who are in the U.S. illegally. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Starbucks offering free coffee Monday after the Super Bowl: How to get yours
item.description: Starbucks is offering a free pick-me-up on Monday for football fans who might be staying up late celebrating (or fuming over) the results of the Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Jane Alvarez-Wertz, Fox 5

item.title: Trump's DOJ drops challenge to gender-affirming care bans but urges Supreme Court to issue decision
item.description: President Trump’s Justice Department on Friday abandoned the Biden administration’s Supreme Court challenge to gender-affirming care bans for minors, but the new administration urged the justices to still resolve the issue this term.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration orders federal agencies to provide lists of underperforming employees
item.description: President Donald Trump's administration has ordered all federal departments and agencies to provide lists of employees who are underperforming, as it seeks to shrink the workforce and awaits a court ruling related to its deferred resignation offers.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Members of Congress denied access to Department of Education
item.description: Multiple members of Congress were denied entry to the Department of Education on Friday, according to videos and social media posts from lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Red states pursue their own DOGE-style reforms
item.description: Numerous Republican-led states are seeking to implement measures to slash the size and cost of state governments in an effort to mirror the Trump administration’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he'll sign order promoting plastic straws
item.description: President Donald Trump on Friday said he plans to sign an executive order promoting plastic straws after his predecessor sought to phase out single-use plastics from government operations.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Expect Super Bowl victory celebrations to look different after last year's deadly shooting
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Expect more security — and nerves — at this year's Super Bowl victory celebration regardless of who wins Sunday’s matchup in New Orleans between the Kansas City Chiefs and Philadelphia Eagles.
item.dc:creator: HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH and MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Arab, Muslim Americans blast Trump's 'grotesque' Gaza proposal
item.description: Arab American and Muslim leaders are blasting President Trump’s proposal to take over Gaza, warning that it’s antithetical to their beliefs and amounts to ethnic cleansing.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Dog Show 101: What's what at the Westminster Kennel Club
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Westminster Kennel Club dog show is back for a 149th year — and back at New York's Madison Square Garden for the first time since early 2020.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Samsung S25 Ultra is the best Galaxy yet
item.description: Ever since I got my hands on the Samsung Galaxy S25 Ultra a few weeks ago, I haven't stopped using it. I test out a lot of phones each year, and this is a sign that this is the best Galaxy smartphone Samsung has ever made.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Man punched window on Frontier flight from Denver: Police
item.description: Passengers on a Frontier Airlines flight leaving Denver for Houston restrained a man after he became combative.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ appeals block of birthright citizenship executive order
item.description: It's the first Justice Department appeal of a lawsuit challenging major Trump administration actions.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: DOGE staffer who accessed Treasury Department payment systems resigns after racist posts exposed
item.description: “Normalize Indian hate,” the account associated with Marko Elez posted in September, regarding people of Indian ethnicity who work in the U.S. tech sector, the Wall Street Journal reported.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, Fox 5

item.title: Alabama convict criticizes Democrat politician in last words before being executed for killing woman
item.description: Demetrius Frazier, who was put to death in Alabama, criticized Democrat Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer in his final words, reports say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arizona grandmother indicted in death of epileptic teen kept in makeshift cage
item.description: The grandmother of a teenage girl with epilepsy who died after police said she had been locked in a makeshift cage for extended periods of time has been indicted by a grand jury in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alabama puts man to death for a 1991 murder in the nation's fourth execution using nitrogen gas
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — An man convicted of murdering a woman after breaking into her apartment as she slept was put to death Thursday evening in Alabama in the nation’s fourth execution using nitrogen gas. Demetrius Frazier, 52, was pronounced dead at 6:36 p.m.
item.dc:creator: KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 500,000 LG electric ovens recalled after fires, pet deaths
item.description: There are 86 reports of unintentional activation of the front knobs, 28 fires, at least eight injuries, and three pet deaths, per the Consumer Product Safety Commission.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, Fox 5

item.title: 13 states to sue over DOGE access to government payment systems containing personal data
item.description: Democratic attorneys general in several states vowed Thursday to file a lawsuit to stop Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency from accessing federal payment systems containing Americans' sensitive personal information.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Crayola brings back 8 retired colors: Here's what they are
item.description: Crayola is going back to the drawing board with its latest collection, bringing back a special assortment of retired colors for the first time in its 122-year history.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump attends bipartisan National Prayer Breakfast, a decadeslong Washington tradition
item.description: President Donald Trump participated in the National Prayer Breakfast at the Capitol on Thursday, joining a more than 70-year-old Washington tradition that brings together a bipartisan group of lawmakers for fellowship.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump attends bipartisan National Prayer Breakfast, a decadeslong Washington tradition
item.description: President Donald Trump participated in the National Prayer Breakfast at the Capitol on Thursday, joining a more than 70-year-old Washington tradition that brings together a bipartisan group of lawmakers for fellowship.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 5 things to watch as the federal buyout deadline approaches
item.description: As the deadline approaches, agencies have amped up the pressure on employees, sending frequent “reminders” and a copy of the contract they would have to sign to accept the deferred resignation.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, WGN 9

item.title: Blake Lively is sued by Texas crisis specialist in latest 'It Ends With Us' lawsuit
item.description: A Texas crisis communications specialist has sued Blake Lively for defamation after the actor pulled him into her legal fight with co-star and director Justin Baldoni over their film, “It Ends With Us.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Storm threatens significant ice accumulations in several mid-Atlantic states, forecasters say
item.description: CHARLESTON, W.Va. (AP) — A winter storm approaching several mid-Atlantic states is threatening significant ice accumulations that could cause widespread power outages and disrupt travel, forecasters warned Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump’s birthright citizenship order is put on hold by a second federal judge
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday ordered a second nationwide pause on President Donald Trump’s executive order seeking to end birthright citizenship for anyone born in the U.S. to someone in the country illegally, calling citizenship a “most precious right.” U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Oklahoma board denies clemency for man on death row, clears path for March execution
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — An Oklahoma board rejected clemency on Wednesday for a man sentenced to die for fatally shooting a woman during a 2005 home-invasion robbery, clearing the way for him to become the first person executed in the state this year.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Suspect arrested after one dead, 5 injured in New Albany mass shooting
item.description: For the latest updates in New Albany warehouse shooting, follow this link. NEW ALBANY, Ohio (WCMH) -- A man is in custody after one person was shot dead and five others were injured after a shooting Tuesday night at a New Albany warehouse. According to authorities, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brett Mills, ABC 4

item.title: Armed teen arrested at Arkansas airport after allegedly demanding plane
item.description: After the employees got to safety, the suspect "breached through a door onto the airfield," an airport official said.
item.dc:creator: Hunter Trombetta, WGN 9

item.title: USPS lifts suspension of Hong Kong and China parcels
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service reversed course Wednesday, saying it would continue to accept all inbound mail and packages from China and Hong Kong.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: USAID direct hires put on leave worldwide, except those deemed essential
item.description: The Trump administration is placing U.S. Agency for International Development direct-hire staffers around the world on leave, except those deemed essential.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: China hits US with retaliatory tariffs
item.description: China hit the U.S. back with retaliatory tariffs across the board over President Trump’s enforcement of tariffs on Chinese goods that went into effect on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9
